missing_areas: Funds / money movement: no coverage of deposit, withdrawal, ACH/wire/crypto, or balance-hold endpoints. A production orchestrator cannot manage its capital lifecycle (funding, sweeping, settlement of cash) from what is documented. Only Get Balance (read-only) is covered.

Subaccount lifecycle and inter-account transfers: the subaccount param (0-63) is threaded through nearly every order/portfolio endpoint, and client_transfer_id is cited as the transfer idempotency key, but the actual endpoints to create/list subaccounts and execute transfers between them are never documented.

RFQ and Quotes REST API: only the WebSocket 'communications' channel and the FIX RFQ session (KalshiRFQ) are covered. The REST create-RFQ / create-quote / accept-quote / list-RFQ endpoints are entirely absent, yet they are the documented pricing path for combos/multivariate and large size.
Block trades: is_block_trade appears on trades/fills and 'block-trade accepts hit the write bucket' is noted, but there are no create-block-trade / accept-block-trade endpoint definitions.

Order groups REST API: order_group_id is an order param, WS exposes order_group_updates, and FIX has OrderGroupRequest/Response (UOG/UOH), but the REST endpoints to create/reset/delete/update an order group and set its contracts_limit are not documented.

Perpetuals (Perps / margined) product line: separate perps_openapi.yaml / perps_asyncapi.yaml specs, the margined exchange_instance, the /account/limits/perps lane, and perps order/market semantics are repeatedly flagged but never mapped. A combined orchestrator that touches perps has zero schema/host/order-model coverage.

Margin / leverage: the FIX Margin API (/fix-margin/*) and a parallel margin-ws WebSocket are referenced but unmapped. Margin requirements, maintenance margin, and liquidation behavior are not covered.

Position and risk limits: per-market position caps, per-member exposure limits, and per-order size caps (and how the exchange rejects breaches) are not covered beyond the single 200,000 open-orders-per-user limit. Risk gating is core to an orchestrator.
Collateral / netting control: netting_enabled behavior (off by default, locked at first order in an event) is described, but the API to read or set it per event is not documented.

Tick-size / price-increment rules: price_level_structure (linear_cent / deci_cent / tapered_deci_cent) and price_ranges[].step exist as fields, but the actual valid price increments each structure implies and how to round a desired price to a legal tick are not specified — directly load-bearing for order placement.

Fixed-point arithmetic and rounding rules: the *_dollars (up to 6dp) vs *_fp (2dp) split is described, but the exact precision/rounding mode for constructing order prices/sizes, and for replicating Kalshi's fee ceil-to-cent on the order total, is not specified. Naive float math will desync.

End-to-end combo/multivariate trading flow: collection lookup -> create-market-in-collection -> RFQ -> quote -> fill is only partially sketched; the operational order/quote sequence and failure modes for combos are thin.
Tax / statements / reporting endpoints (1099s, monthly statements, trade-confirm documents) — not covered; relevant for production bookkeeping/reconciliation.

Settlement edge cases and operations: disputes, amendments (status 'disputed'/'amended'), provisional markets (is_provisional), early-close conditions, and the dispute/amend notification path are listed as fields but have no operational handling guidance.
unverified_claims: Whether the legacy V1 /portfolio/orders* endpoints are still live as of today (2026-06-13). Deprecation is 'no earlier than May 21 2026' and the rate-cost escalation (10x->100x) completes June 4 2026 — so they may already be effectively unusable or removed. The orchestrator's entire order path depends on this.

client_order_id idempotency/dedup guarantee: the quick-start says duplicates are rejected, but the Create Order and Batch Create reference pages document NO hard dedup guarantee or time window. This is the single most load-bearing fact for safe order retries and is contradictory across pages.

side/action field removal: the changelog says 'not removed before May 28 2026' while the get-fills and get-orders/order pages say 'May 14 2026'. Today is 2026-06-13, so under either reading they may already be gone — code reading side/action could break. Unresolved.

V1 Create Order token cost: the create-order page literally reads '100 tokens per request' while batch-create bills 10/order and the generic default is 10. Whether 100 is a static cost or the in-progress legacy escalation is unconfirmed — affects write-budget sizing.
Default time_in_force when omitted in V1 Create Order is not documented (commonly GTC in practice, unconfirmed in spec).

min_ts/max_ts unit on Get Fills/Orders/Settlements: docs say 'Unix timestamp' without stating seconds vs milliseconds (auth header is ms, historical filters were seconds). Unverified and easy to get wrong.

429 response body shape: rate_limits page says {"error":"too many requests"} while per-endpoint OpenAPI lists 429 under the generic {code,message,details?,service?} envelope. Which actually ships is unverified — affects 429 parsing/backoff.
RSA private key PEM variant (PKCS1 vs PKCS8) and required RSA key size are not stated ('RSA_PRIVATE_KEY' suggests PKCS1 but unconfirmed) — load-bearing for the signing implementation.
REST clock-skew / timestamp validity window is undocumented (FIX is explicitly 30s; REST unknown). Determines how tight NTP sync must be and retry behavior on signature-time errors.
Maximum batch size for Batch Create / Batch Cancel: only 'scales with your tier's write budget' — no hard integer cap. Cannot size batches deterministically.
Whether the perps/margined lane (/account/limits/perps) uses the same tier budget numbers as Predictions or a separate schedule — endpoint exists but schema/numbers unconfirmed.
Exact HTTP status + error code returned when placing/amending an order during a trading pause or exchange pause (e.g. 409 vs 400, 'market_inactive' vs 'exchange_closed') — unspecified.

Full REST error 'code' string enum and their HTTP-status mappings are not on any single page; only fragments (the 2026-01-26 order-validation codes) are confirmed. The fix/error-handling page is FIX-only.
Whether the announced 'cannot specify both immediate_or_cancel and expiration_ts' breaking change is currently enforced (changelog release was TBD).
Max candlesticks per request: event candlesticks reference maxAggregateCandidates / adjusted_end_ts but the numeric cap is never stated; per-market/batch/historical caps undocumented.

Whether seq increments on every WS data channel (ticker/trade/fill/market_positions) or only orderbook_delta and order_group_updates — gap-detection logic depends on this and is only partially confirmed.

Legacy host retirement: no sunset date for api.elections.kalshi.com / demo-api.kalshi.co, and the older trading-api.kalshi.com status is inferred from absence only. Whether the legacy WS host wss://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 still accepts connections is unconfirmed.

SDK currency: published SDKs are v3.20.0 (OpenAPI Generator 7.17.0) but the spec is already 3.21.0; the 3.21.0 SDK ship date and any schema drift are unknown. TS class names were verified via npm metadata, not actual runtime usage.
recommended_followups: Fetch and parse the full https://docs.kalshi.com/openapi.yaml (spec 3.21.0) to enumerate EVERY endpoint and component, specifically to recover the missing funds/transfer, subaccount, RFQ/quote, block-trade, and order-group REST routes and the complete error-code enum in one pass.

Read the Perps product docs: https://docs.kalshi.com/welcome (Perps section) plus perps_openapi.yaml and perps_asyncapi.yaml, and the /api-reference for /account/limits/perps, to map perps hosts, order model, and rate buckets.
Read the FIX Margin API pages under https://docs.kalshi.com/fix-margin/* (authentication, connectivity, order-entry, order-groups) and the margin-ws WebSocket docs to map margin/leverage trading.
Read https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/maintenance_and_pauses in full for the exact maintenance schedule and the documented REST behavior (status/error) during trading vs exchange pauses.
Find and read the Kalshi position-limits / risk-limits documentation (help center + any /getting_started page) to capture per-market position caps and per-member exposure enforcement.

Locate the subaccount and transfer reference pages (search api-reference for 'transfer' / client_transfer_id) and the RFQ/quote and block-trade reference pages to fill the missing write-path endpoints.

DEMO EXPERIMENT — generate a demo API key and inspect the downloaded .key PEM header ('BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY' = PKCS1 vs 'BEGIN PRIVATE KEY' = PKCS8) and modulus size to lock the signing implementation.
DEMO EXPERIMENT — submit the SAME client_order_id twice (with and without a partial fill in between) against demo to empirically determine dedup behavior, response, and any time window.

DEMO EXPERIMENT — POST a legacy V1 /portfolio/orders order against demo to confirm it is still live and capture its actual token cost via GET /trade-api/v2/account/endpoint_costs and GET /trade-api/v2/account/limits before/after.

DEMO EXPERIMENT — query GET /portfolio/fills with min_ts/max_ts in seconds vs milliseconds to determine which unit filters correctly; also inspect a live order/fill response to confirm whether deprecated side/action fields are still present (resolving the May 14 vs May 28 conflict).

DEMO EXPERIMENT — trigger a 429 (burst past the write budget) and capture the exact body and headers to resolve the {"error":"too many requests"} vs generic-envelope conflict and confirm absence of Retry-After.
DEMO EXPERIMENT — send a deliberately skewed KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP (e.g. +5s, +60s, +5min) to a private REST endpoint to measure the actual clock-skew tolerance window.

DEMO EXPERIMENT — GET /markets/{ticker} for several real markets across categories to capture concrete price_level_structure / price_ranges[].step values and derive the valid tick increments per structure; confirm whether sub-penny ticks appear.

DEMO EXPERIMENT — confirm the demo orderbook endpoints (single + batch) require the same auth as production, and open a WebSocket to observe whether seq is present on ticker/trade/fill/market_positions envelopes.

Read https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/account/list-non-default-endpoint-costs live response and GET /account/api_usage_level/volume_progress to capture the dynamic per-route cost table and tier-earn thresholds the orchestrator must budget against.
